deep_dive.title: DDIAgents: Mechanism-Conditioned Context Flow for Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Drug-drug interaction (DDI) prediction is essential for medication safety, yet it requires reasoning over heterogeneous biomedical evidence whose relevance changes across interaction mechanisms. We propose DDIAgents, a mechanism-conditioned multi-agent framework that performs DDI prediction through dynamic knowledge orchestration. Given a drug pair, a planner agent instantiates specialized expert agents, routes mechanism-relevant knowledge sources to each agent, and aggregates their analyses through a conclusion agent. By adapting context flow to the inferred interaction mechanism, DDIAgents reduces irrelevant information, supports complementary expert reasoning, and produces interpretable agent-level rationales. Extensive experiments on realistic DDI prediction benchmarks show that DDIAgents consistently outperforms existing feature-based, graph-based, LLM-based, and agent-based baselines. Beyond prediction performance, DDIAgents demonstrates how multi-agent systems can organize heterogeneous scientific knowledge for adaptive and interpretable AI4Science reasoning.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: drug-drug interaction prediction using multi-agent framework with mechanism-conditioned context flow
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用机制条件上下文流的多智能体框架进行药物相互作用预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用机制条件上下文流的多智能体框架进行药物相互作用预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用机制条件上下文流的多智能体框架进行药物相互作用预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DDIAgents is a mechanism-conditioned multi-agent framework for drug-drug interaction prediction that dynamically routes relevant biomedical knowledge based on inferred interaction mechanism, reducing irrelevant information and producing interpretable rationales.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 药物相互作用(DDI)预测依赖多种生物医学证据，但其相关性随交互机制变化。现有方法缺乏机制感知的上下文管理。本文提出DDIAgents，一种机制条件化多智能体框架，由规划器根据药物对推断机制，路由相关专业知识到各专家智能体，最后聚合分析。实验表明该方法减少了信息冗余，支持互补推理，并输出可解释理由。该工作为DDI预测提供了一种动态知识编排的新范式，有望应用于冷启动场景。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Triplet Plausibility: Relation Set Completion in Knowledge Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Knowledge graphs (KGs) organize real-world knowledge as triplets and underpin many downstream applications. Due to their inherent incompleteness, knowledge graph completion (KGC) is widely studied and is typically formulated as triplet prediction, with link prediction as the dominant paradigm. However, this formulation focuses on the incompleteness of triplet-wise information and overlooks the incompleteness of entity-relation compatibility information. To address this limitation, we introduce a relation set completion task (RSC), which complements the link prediction task and aims to reason about missing relations that are semantically compatible with a given entity. We further propose a Relation Set Embedding model (RelSetE), which models latent patterns among the observed relations of entities to infer missing ones. To evaluate RelSetE, we derive three benchmark datasets from standard KG benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RelSetE effectively captures entity-relation compatibility patterns and performs favorably in inferring missing relations of entities. Code and data are publicly available.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: knowledge graph completion method applicable to drug discovery
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 知识图谱补全方法可用于药物发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 知识图谱补全方法可用于药物发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 知识图谱补全方法可用于药物发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes relation set completion (RSC) for KGs, complementing link prediction; models latent compatibility of relations with entities via RelSetE.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对知识图谱中三元组补全忽略实体-关系兼容性的问题，提出了关系集补全任务（RSC）及RelSetE模型，通过建模实体与关系的潜在兼容性来推理缺失关系。该方法作为链路预测的补充，可提升知识图谱的完整性，有望应用于药物发现中的关系推断。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对知识图谱中三元组补全忽略实体-关系兼容性的问题，提出了关系集补全任务（RSC）及RelSetE模型，通过建模实体与关系的潜在兼容性来推理缺失关系。该方法作为链路预测的补充，可提升知识图谱的完整性，有望应用于药物发现中的关系推断。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Triplet Plausibility: Relation Set Completion in Knowledge Graphs
quick_skim.abstract: Knowledge graphs (KGs) organize real-world knowledge as triplets and underpin many downstream applications. Due to their inherent incompleteness, knowledge graph completion (KGC) is widely studied and is typically formulated as triplet prediction, with link prediction as the dominant paradigm. However, this formulation focuses on the incompleteness of triplet-wise information and overlooks the incompleteness of entity-relation compatibility information. To address this limitation, we introduce a relation set completion task (RSC), which complements the link prediction task and aims to reason about missing relations that are semantically compatible with a given entity. We further propose a Relation Set Embedding model (RelSetE), which models latent patterns among the observed relations of entities to infer missing ones. To evaluate RelSetE, we derive three benchmark datasets from standard KG benchmarks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that RelSetE effectively captures entity-relation compatibility patterns and performs favorably in inferring missing relations of entities. Code and data are publicly available.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes relation set completion for KGs, directly applicable to drug discovery KGC
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出知识图谱关系集补全，直接可应用于药物发现中的知识图谱补全

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces relation set completion (RSC) task and RelSetE model for completing missing relations in KGs, complementing link prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文提出关系集补全（RSC）任务，以补充链接预测的不足，并设计RelSetE模型来推理与实体语义兼容的缺失关系。该方法可迁移至药物发现中的知识图谱补全，如预测药物-药物相互作用。

quick_skim.title: PromptGNN-sim: Deep Fusion and Alignment of GNN and LLMs for Text-Attributed Graph Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Text-Attributed Graphs (TAGs) combine textual semantics with graph structure and are central to many graph learning tasks. However, existing fusion methods often treat text and structure as separate inputs in a shallow, one-way pipeline, which limits deep interaction between modalities and weakens performance under sparse connectivity or cross-graph generalisation. To address this issue, we propose PromptGNN-sim, a bi-directional structure-semantic fusion framework for collaborative GNN-LLM learning. PromptGNN-sim uses a Graph Attention Network (GAT) for semantically aware neighborhood selection by combining structural attention with textual similarity. The selected structural context is then used to generate structure-aware prompts for an LLM, including the target node summary, label categories, and representative keywords from similar neighbors. During training, bi-directional cross-modal contrastive learning and cross-attention are introduced to jointly optimize the GNN and LLM components. Experiments on six public datasets, including Cora, Pubmed, and WikiCS, evaluate accuracy, generalisation, and robustness under cross-task transfer, cross-dataset generalisation, and sparse perturbations. Results show that PromptGNN-sim outperforms classical GNNs, LLMs, and recent GNN-LLM fusion methods, demonstrating the effectiveness of interactive structure-semantic collaboration for text-attributed graph learning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Deep fusion of GNN and LLM for text-attributed graph learning, applicable to DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: GNN与LLM的深度融合用于文本属性图学习，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: GNN与LLM的深度融合用于文本属性图学习，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: GNN与LLM的深度融合用于文本属性图学习，可应用于药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PromptGNN-sim, a bi-directional fusion of GNN and LLM for text-attributed graph learning, using structure-aware prompts to improve performance on sparse graphs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有GNN-LLM融合方法交互浅层、稀疏图效果差的问题，提出PromptGNN-sim框架，通过双向结构-语义融合和结构感知提示，在多个文本属性图数据集上提升了节点分类和链接预测性能，为药物相互作用等图任务提供了可迁移方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有GNN-LLM融合方法交互浅层、稀疏图效果差的问题，提出PromptGNN-sim框架，通过双向结构-语义融合和结构感知提示，在多个文本属性图数据集上提升了节点分类和链接预测性能，为药物相互作用等图任务提供了可迁移方法。
